channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nikon finally gives its beginner mirrorless cameras a boost with two excellent new lenses, including its first crop sensor macro
item.description: Nikon has launched two APS-C format DX lenses; the 35mm f/1.7 macro and 16-50mm f/2.8 zoom – two solid looking lenses for a system that had previously lacked investment
item.dc:creator: Peter Fenech, TechRadar

item.title: Former Google CEO warns AI systems can be hacked to become extremely dangerous weapons
item.description: Eric Schmidt alerts that hackers can reverse-engineer AI models to bypass safety measures, citing examples like the jailbroken ChatGPT variant called DAN.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the winners of Instagram’s inaugural award for groundbreaking creators
item.description: A makeup illusionist, a photography project, and an innovative DJ are among the winners of Instagram’s inaugural Rings awards.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft sounds alarm as hackers turn Teams platform into 'real-world dangers' for users
item.description: Cybercriminals exploit Microsoft Teams through impersonation, malicious links and fake profiles to gather intel and deliver ransomware to personal and work devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to make workers happier with less pay
item.description: Workers want remote work so badly they’re willing to take a 25% pay cut to get it, according to a new study from economists at Harvard University, Brown University, and UCLA. The study published this week was conducted with Levels.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta adds parental controls for AI-teen interactions
item.description: Meta is adding parental controls to kids’ interactions with artificial intelligence chatbots — including the ability to turn off one-on-one chats with AI characters altogether.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia sharing tips on curbing social media for children before age limit starts in December
item.description: The Australian government has begun a public education campaign with tips on how to wean children off social media ahead of a world-first national 16-year age limit taking effect.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Uber wants to become the ‘Uber’ of data labeling
item.description: Following its entrance into the data labeling space last year with the launch of a new division, Scaled Solutions, Uber on Thursday introduced a pilot project in the US market that will see its drivers, like their counterparts in India, annotate data for use in AI training.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Getac launches world's fastest rugged laptop - and, for the first time in decades, we have an AMD-powered rugged notebook
item.description: Getac’s S510AD introduces AMD’s AI power to rugged laptops, blending durability, sustainability, and field-ready performance for professional users.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Chamber of Commerce sues to block $100K H-1B visa fees
item.description: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce sued the Trump administration Thursday over its decision to raise the fee for H-1B work visa applications to $100,000, arguing that President Trump exceeded his authority in altering the visa program.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Unions sue Trump administration over social media vetting of visa holders
item.description: Three labor unions sued the Trump administration Thursday in connection with a program that searched social media posts of visa holders, arguing that it violates the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Major companies, including Google and Dior, hit by massive Salesforce data breach
item.description: Google, Dior, Allianz and dozens of other companies lost sensitive customer data in Salesforce-related breaches affecting millions of records across multiple sectors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Energy Department offers $1.6 billion loan guarantee to upgrade transmission lines across Midwest
item.description: The Department of Energy says it has finalized a $1.6 billion loan guarantee to a subsidiary of one of the nation’s largest power companies to upgrade nearly 5,000 miles of transmission lines across five states, mostly in the Midwest, for largely fossil fuel-run energy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anthropic releases new version of its smaller Haiku model
item.description: Anthropic is launching Claude Haiku 4.5, the newest version of the generative AI ​(genAI) ​company’s smallest language model. The company says it offers the same performance as Sonnet 4, but at a third of the cost and more than twice the speed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Rethinking operations in an agentic AI world
item.description: IT operations have long been defined by one overriding goal: keep applications running at all costs. Success was measured in uptime. Agentic AI systems break that assumption.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Shaping Tomorrow’s Leaders: Mentorship in Action
item.description: Strong mentorship builds strong leaders. Join us on Oct. 28 at 2 p.m. ET to hear how agencies are transforming mentorship to boost connection, engagement, and career growth across the workforce. The post Shaping Tomorrow’s Leaders: Mentorship in Action first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: DOGE’s arrival at Social Security sparked chaos and missed a chance for real reform
item.description: "There's a real brain drain that's happened there that I think they're still grappling with," said Eli Hager. The post DOGE’s arrival at Social Security sparked chaos and missed a chance for real reform first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: US indicts 'mastermind' of Cambodian cyberscam network, seizes $15B in Bitcoin
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Tuesday it has indicted the alleged orchestrator of a Cambodia-based cryptocurrency fraud scheme. The indictment, filed Oct.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI Codex rivals Claude Code
item.description: Codex CLI is an open-source coding agent from OpenAI, written primarily in Rust, that runs locally on your computer. Codex IDE extension is a coding agent that runs in Visual Studio Code and its forks, such as Cursor and Windsurf.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation
